homepage.faq.titleAccent.message: Everything You Want to Know is Here

homepage.faq.q1.message: Which macOS versions does LinguaX support?

homepage.faq.a1.message: LinguaX supports macOS 13.0 Ventura and higher, perfectly compatible with all Mac devices with Intel and Apple Silicon chips. Specially optimized for the latest macOS systems.

homepage.faq.q2.message: Will it affect system performance as a menu bar app?

homepage.faq.a2.message: Absolutely not. LinguaX uses efficient lightweight architecture, app size around 10MB, extremely low memory usage, almost zero CPU usage. Specially optimized for menu bar environment, won't affect system performance.

homepage.faq.a3.message: LinguaX provides two configuration methods: app binding and domain binding. You can assign dedicated input methods for each app, or configure input methods for different website domains. With accessibility permission enabled, switching browser tabs will auto-switch input method based on domain.

homepage.faq.q4.message: What is Accessibility Mode? Why do I need to enable it?

homepage.faq.a4.message: Accessibility Mode is a system permission provided by macOS. Once enabled, LinguaX can detect the current website domain in browsers. This allows more granular control: when switching between browser tabs, input method automatically switches based on the domain you configured.

homepage.faq.q5.message: How is data security and privacy guaranteed?

homepage.faq.a5.message: LinguaX strictly follows Apple's privacy policy, all configuration data is stored locally, never collects or uploads any personal information. Accessibility permission is only used to read browser tab information, ensuring your privacy is absolutely safe.

homepage.cta.description.message: Join nearly two thousand users and experience the most natural input method switching.

homepage.cta.descriptionStrong.message: Free trial, no ads, privacy protection, continuous updates.

homepage.features.mouse.description.message: Smooth scrolling and reverse scrolling for third-party mice, experience Magic Mouse-like fluidity

homepage.faq.q6.message: What is Mouse Enhancement? Do I need this feature?

homepage.faq.a6.message: Mouse Enhancement is designed for third-party mouse users. It provides smooth scrolling that simulates trackpad inertia for a silky natural feel, and reverse scrolling that controls mouse wheel direction independently while keeping trackpad natural scrolling direction. If you use a third-party mouse and want a better scrolling experience, this feature is perfect for you.

homepage.faq.q7.message: Does the lifetime license support refunds?

homepage.faq.a7.message: We offer a 3-day no-questions-asked refund guarantee. We want every user to be satisfied with LinguaX.

landing.home.meta.title.message: LinguaX – Mouse Enhancement, Push-to-Talk & Input Automation for macOS

landing.home.meta.description.message: LinguaX gives third-party mice a pro macOS feel — smooth scrolling, gesture and side-button mapping, and push-to-talk voice typing — plus automatic input-source switching. Free trial, $9.9 lifetime.

landing.home.shots.card2.alt.message: LinguaX app and input source rule mapping

landing.home.hero.chip.message: macOS 13+ · Apple Silicon & Intel · Menu bar app

landing.home.hero.description.message: LinguaX is a macOS utility for mouse enhancement, push-to-talk voice input, and automatic input-source switching — smooth scrolling, gesture and button mapping, and input source rules by app and website.

landing.home.note.message: Pricing: free trial, then a one-time Lifetime purchase ($9.9), no subscription.

landing.home.pain.title.message: Small frictions that add up every day

landing.home.pain.card1.title.message: Third-party mice never quite feel right

landing.home.pain.card1.description.message: Scrolling feels choppy, inertia is off, and macOS gives you almost no tuning controls for non-Apple mice.

landing.home.pain.card2.title.message: Side buttons are wasted real estate

landing.home.pain.card2.description.message: Most third-party button mapping tools are clunky or app-unaware. You end up ignoring the buttons.

landing.home.pain.card3.title.message: Voice and language switching still breaks focus

landing.home.pain.card3.description.message: Reaching for a key to start dictation, then manually switching input source — every single time.

landing.home.diff.title.message: What LinguaX does for your mouse and typing

landing.home.diff.card1.description.message: Tunable, smooth scrolling for any mouse, with per-app overrides when one app needs a different feel.

landing.home.diff.card2.title.message: Gestures and buttons that do real work

landing.home.diff.card2.description.message: Map clicks, long-press, and directional drags. Hold a side button to push-to-talk and start voice dictation without leaving the mouse.

landing.home.diff.card3.title.message: Type in the right context, automatically

landing.home.diff.card3.description.message: App and website-domain rules switch your input source for you, so you stop correcting your keyboard mid-sentence.

landing.home.steps.title.message: Start small, feel the difference quickly

landing.home.steps.card1.description.message: Enable smooth scrolling and map one mouse action you will actually use every day.

landing.home.steps.card2.title.message: Add a small input automation layer

landing.home.steps.card2.description.message: Set one app rule and one domain rule to remove the most common typing interruptions.

landing.home.steps.card3.description.message: Use diagnostics and app-scoped overrides only when needed, and keep your setup easy to maintain.

landing.home.shots.card1.caption.message: Overview with rule and device status

landing.home.shots.card3.caption.message: Mouse+ feel tuning and gesture entry

landing.home.audience.item1.message: People using third-party mice who want smoother scrolling and more reliable button control on macOS.

landing.home.audience.item2.message: Developers, designers, and operators who rely on repeatable mouse and keyboard actions daily.

landing.home.audience.item3.message: Multilingual users who also want input automation without adding extra complexity.

landing.home.compat.description.message: A native menu-bar app that respects your machine and your data.

landing.home.compat.signal1.description.message: Written in Swift, not Electron — roughly 1/50 the size of Logi Options+. No driver install needed.

landing.home.compat.signal2.description.message: Download and use it. 30-day full-feature trial, no sign-up, one-time purchase.

landing.home.compat.signal3.description.message: No tracking, no analytics. We can't see how you use it.

landing.home.compat.signal4.description.message: Rules stored locally on macOS 13+ (Apple Silicon & Intel). Optional iCloud sync when you turn it on.

landing.home.cta.description.message: Start with a full-feature trial. It is a one-time purchase with no subscription, and your rules stay on your Mac.

landing.home.faq.q1.message: Is LinguaX mainly for mouse enhancement?

landing.home.faq.a1.message: Mouse+ is what we lead with — smooth scrolling, gesture/button mapping, and per-app overrides. But push-to-talk voice input and automatic input source switching are equally core capabilities, not add-ons. You get all three.

landing.home.faq.q2.message: Do I still get input automation?

landing.home.faq.a2.message: Yes. LinguaX also provides app and domain input switching to keep typing aligned with your active context.

landing.home.faq.a3.message: Start with a 30-day full-feature trial, then upgrade to a $9.9 one-time Lifetime license only if you want to keep using it.

landing.home.faq.a4.message: Usually no. Most users can get a useful first setup in a few minutes and refine later if needed.

landing.home.mouseplus.description.message: Everything your third-party mouse needs to feel like it belongs on macOS.

landing.home.mouseplus.block.description.message: Works with any USB or Bluetooth mouse. No driver install needed — just LinguaX.

landing.home.mouseplus.sub1.description.message: Tunable scroll curve for any mouse, with fine-grained controls for step size, speed gain, and inertia duration. Per-app overrides when one app needs the smooth scrolling toggled.

landing.home.mouseplus.sub2.description.message: Map clicks, long-press, and directional drags to real actions: app launch, system controls, media, or custom scripts.

landing.home.mouseplus.sub3.description.message: Override any button or scroll behavior for a specific app without touching the global config.

landing.home.mouseplus.shot1.alt.message: LinguaX Mouse+ smooth scrolling and feel tuning

landing.home.mouseplus.shot2.alt.message: LinguaX Mouse+ button mapping and app overrides

landing.home.ptt.description.message: Hold a mouse side button to start voice dictation. Release to stop. Your hands never leave the mouse.

landing.home.ptt.block.description.message: LinguaX maps a mouse side button to hold the macOS Fn / Globe key. While held, macOS Dictation activates. Release the button and dictation stops — just like a walkie-talkie.

landing.home.ptt.step1.message: Map any side button to "Hold Globe Key" in the Mapping panel

landing.home.ptt.step2.message: Hold the button — macOS Dictation starts immediately

landing.home.ptt.step3.message: Release — dictation stops. No keyboard required.

landing.home.input.block.title.message: Your input context follows your focus

landing.home.input.block.p1.message: Set one rule per app and one per domain. LinguaX switches input source automatically whenever context changes.

landing.home.input.block.p2.message: Falls back to your default input source when no rule matches. Works in the background, invisible when working correctly.

landing.home.faq.a5.message: Any USB or Bluetooth mouse. No brand restriction, no driver needed.

pairTool.meta.title.message: Pair a Logitech Bolt / Unifying / Lightspeed receiver in your browser
pairTool.meta.title.description: HTML <title> for the pair-receiver tool page

pairTool.meta.description.message: Pair, list, and unpair devices on a Logitech Bolt, Unifying, or Lightspeed receiver — right in Chrome or Edge, no Logitech software required.
pairTool.meta.description.description: HTML <meta description> for the pair-receiver tool page

pairTool.faq.support.q.message: Which receivers does this tool support?

pairTool.faq.support.a.message: Logi Bolt, Logitech Unifying, and Lightspeed (G-series) receivers. It pairs, lists, and unpairs devices. Firmware updates and dongle replacement are out of scope — use Logitech’s own tools for those.

pairTool.faq.browser.q.message: Why does it need Chrome or Edge?

pairTool.faq.browser.a.message: The tool talks to the receiver over WebHID, a browser API that Safari and Firefox don’t implement. Nothing is installed and nothing leaves your machine — the protocol runs entirely between your browser and the receiver.

pairTool.faq.slots.q.message: My receiver has no free slots. What do I do?

pairTool.faq.slots.a.message: Unifying and Bolt receivers hold up to six devices. Connect the receiver, open "See paired devices", and unpair one you no longer use — the slot frees up immediately.

pairTool.faq.official.q.message: Is this an official Logitech tool?

pairTool.faq.official.a.message: No. It is an independent implementation of the publicly documented HID++ protocol, offered by LinguaX as a convenience. For Logitech’s own web pairing tool, see logiwebconnect.com.

pairTool.faq.brands.q.message: What about Razer, SteelSeries, or other brands?

pairTool.faq.brands.a.message: Logitech is the only vendor with a publicly documented receiver-pairing protocol (HID++), which is what makes this tool possible. Other brands’ 2.4GHz dongles use undocumented proprietary protocols and usually ship pre-paired, and Bluetooth mice (any brand, including Magic Mouse) pair through macOS System Settings directly. Once connected, LinguaX’s button mapping works with any brand.

pairTool.faq.next.q.message: What do I do after pairing?

pairTool.faq.next.a.message: macOS drives the mouse immediately. To remap side buttons, add gestures, or set up push-to-talk, install LinguaX — a ~10MB native app with a 30-day free trial.

pairTool.steps.3.body.message: Toggle its power switch or connect button.

pairTool.steps.4.body.message: Device name appears in the widget.

pairTool.hero.subhead.message: Bolt, Unifying, and Lightspeed — no Logi Options+, G HUB, or account.

pairTool.cta.headline.message: Paired. Now make the buttons worth it.

pairTool.cta.sub.message: Smooth scrolling, side-button and gesture mapping, per-app behavior, push-to-talk — LinguaX, ~10 MB native, one-time $9.9.

pairWidget.error.timeout.message: No device showed up. Turn the mouse off and on (or press its connect button), then try again.
pairWidget.error.timeout.description: Pair error: timeout waiting for device

pairWidget.error.generic.description: Pair error: generic failure with adapter kind and reason

pairWidget.hint.receiverHint.message: This model usually ships with a {hint} receiver — plug it in, then click below.
pairWidget.hint.receiverHint.description: Hint shown on model pages telling which receiver ships with this device

pairWidget.btn.pairNew.description: Primary button: start pairing a new device

pairWidget.btn.hidePaired.description: Secondary button when list is open

pairWidget.btn.seePaired.description: Secondary button when list is closed

pairWidget.unsupported.message: That device isn’t a supported receiver (Bolt / Unifying / Lightspeed). Pick the small USB receiver, not the mouse itself.
pairWidget.unsupported.description: Shown when the picked HID device isn't a supported Logitech receiver

pairWidget.btn.tryAgain.description: Button to reset and try again

pairWidget.pairing.title.bolt.message: Put your device in pairing mode (press & hold its pair / Easy-Switch button)…
pairWidget.pairing.title.bolt.description: Instruction for Bolt receivers: put device in pairing mode

pairWidget.pairing.title.unifying.message: Now turn on your device, or press its connect / Easy-Switch button…

pairWidget.picker.title.description: Title when multiple Bolt devices are discovered and user must pick one

pairWidget.passkey.keyboard.title.message: On the keyboard, type {digits} and press Enter
pairWidget.passkey.keyboard.title.description: Ask user to type passkey on keyboard

pairWidget.passkey.mouse.title.message: Click your mouse in this sequence:
pairWidget.passkey.mouse.title.description: Ask user to click mouse in L/R sequence

pairWidget.passkey.mouse.finalTitle.message: Now press LEFT + RIGHT buttons at the same time
pairWidget.passkey.mouse.finalTitle.description: Ask user to press both buttons for final confirmation

pairWidget.passkey.mouse.progress.description: Progress counter for mouse click sequence with L/R legend

pairWidget.passkey.mouse.finalHint.message: Final step: press {both} buttons together to confirm
pairWidget.passkey.mouse.finalHint.description: Hint for pressing both mouse buttons

pairWidget.success.title.description: Success message showing paired device name or slot number

pairWidget.success.installHint.message: Now install LinguaX to remap its side buttons — {dl}
pairWidget.success.installHint.description: Post-pair CTA text with LinguaX download link

pairWidget.list.needConnect.message: Connect a receiver first (click “Pair a new device” and pick it) to see its paired devices.
pairWidget.list.needConnect.description: Shown when user asks to see paired devices but adapter isn't ready

pairWidget.list.unpairConfirm.message: Unpair {label}? It will stop working until you pair it again.
pairWidget.list.unpairConfirm.description: Confirm dialog before unpairing a device

pairWidget.list.loading.description: Loading state while reading paired devices

pairWidget.list.empty.message: No devices paired to this receiver — all six slots are free.
